rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: NASA programs and projects that do not meet the provisions of this NASA Technical Standard shall be assessed by the NASA Program Manager for the associated risk to the success of the planned NASA mission and approved by the delegated Technical Authority.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: This NASA Technical Standard shall not supersede applicable laws and regulations unless a specific exemption has been obtained by the Office of the NASA Chief Engineer.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The service environments and limit loads shall be well defined.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Aerospace standard manufacturing and process controls shall be used in hardware fabrication and handling.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Deviations of the test article from the flight configuration shall be documented and approved by the delegated Technical Authority.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: A protoflight test shall be followed by inspection and functionality assessment.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The strength verification program shall be approved by the delegated Technical Authority.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The magnitude of the static test loads shall be equivalent to limit loads multiplied by the qualification, acceptance, or proof test factor.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Strength model verification, if required, shall be accomplished over the entire load range.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The test article shall be instrumented to provide sufficient test data for correlation with the strength model.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Each habitable module, propellant tank, and SRM case shall be proof pressure tested.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Departures from test plans and procedures, including failures that occur during testing or are uncovered as part of post-test inspection, shall be documented by a non- conformance report per the approved quality assurance plan.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: When using the prototype structural verification approach, metallic structures shall be verified to have no detrimental yielding at yield design load before testing to full qualification load levels.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: When using the protoflight structural verification approach, design factors shall be specified to prevent detrimental yielding of the metallic structure or damage to the composite/bonded flight structure during test.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Analysis shall be provided with an acceptable engineering rationale for the "no-test" option.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: To use the "no-test" approach, project-specific criteria and rationale shall be developed for review and approval by the delegated Technical Authority.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Any proposed use of probabilistic criteria to supplement or as an alternative to deterministic factors of safety shall be approved by the delegated Technical Authority on an individual-case basis.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.section_title: Design and Test Factors of Safety
rules.statement: The design factors of safety and test factors of this NASA Technical Standard are the minimum required values for NASA spaceflight structures and shall be applied to the limit stress condition, including additive thermal or pressure stresses.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: If pressure or temperature has a relieving or stabilizing effect on the mode of failure, then for analysis or test of that failure mode, the unfactored stresses induced by temperature or the minimum expected pressure shall be used in conjunction with the factored stresses from all other loads.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Material selection and derivation of material design allowables shall follow the requirements defined in NASA-STD-6016, Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The factored stresses shall not exceed material allowable stresses (yield and ultimate) under the expected temperature, pressure, and other operating conditions.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The hardware shall be designed to preclude any detrimental yielding under limit loads and, where applicable, under protoflight or proof test loads.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Applications of design and test factors to the development and verification of a structure shall be accepted by the delegated Technical Authority only when all the constraints and preconditions specified in section 1.4 are met.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The minimum design and test factors of safety for metallic structures shall be as specified in Table 1, Minimum Design and Test Factors for Metallic Structures.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Workmanship verification shall be performed for the first flight build and follow-on structures under a prototype test approach and for follow-on structures under a protoflight test approach.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The workmanship verification program shall be approved by the delegated Technical Authority.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Threaded fastening systems shall be designed and analyzed per the requirements specified in NASA-STD-5020, Requirements for Threaded Fastening Systems in Spaceflight Hardware.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The minimum design and test factors of safety for metallic fasteners and internally threaded parts shall be as specified in Table

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Composite/bonded structures, including bonded sandwich structures and bonded inserts but excluding glass, developed for NASA spaceflight missions, shall, as a minimum, use the design and test factors specified in Table 2, Minimum Design and Test Factors for Composite/Bonded Structures.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The design and verification of glass and ceramic structures for human-rated spaceflight application shall follow the requirements defined in NASA-STD-5018, Strength Design and Verification Criteria for Glass, Ceramics, and Windows in Human Space Flight Applications.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: A non-fracture-based strength approach is possible for glass and ceramic structures in non-safety critical robotic spaceflight applications, such as mirrors and lenses used in science instruments, provided conservative strength allowables are used; however, approval by the delegated Technical Authority of the allowables and approach shall be obtained before implementing this option.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The minimum design and test factors for pressurized and nonpressurized glass/ceramics shall be as specified in Table 3, Minimum Design and Test Factors for Glass/Ceramics in Robotic Applications.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Structural integrity of all pressurized glass and ceramics shall be verified by both analysis and proof testing.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Proof tests of glass and ceramics shall be configured to simulate flight-like boundary conditions and loading.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: For glass proof testing, the total time during unload shall be as short as possible and the whole test performed in an environment designed to minimize unrealistic flaw growth.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: All glass and ceramic bonds shall be proof tested in the bonded flight assembly.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The design and test factors for structural bonds in glass or ceramics shall be as specified in Table 4, Minimum Design and Test Factors for Bonds in Glass/Ceramic Structures.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Metallic pressure vessels and pressurized components shall be designed, qualified, and accepted per the requirements of ANSI/AIAA S-080, Space Systems - Metallic Pressure Vessels, Pressurized Structures, and Pressurized Components.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Composite overwrapped pressure vessels (COPVs) shall be designed, qualified, and accepted per the requirements of ANSI/AIAA S-081, Space Systems - Composite Overwrapped Pressure Vessels (COPVs).

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: ANSI/AIAA S-080 and S-081 shall be tailored such that MDP is substituted for all references to maximum expected operating pressure (MEOP).

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Habitable modules shall maintain dimensional stability required for functionality of structural and mechanical attachments, pressure connections, and openings for doors or hatches throughout their service life in the applicable environments.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Habitable modules shall withstand applicable loads with the doors or hatches in the open and closed condition for the applicable ground and mission environments.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The minimum design and test factors of safety for habitable modules, doors, and hatches shall be as specified in Table 5, Minimum Design and Test Factors for Habitable Modules, Doors, and Hatches.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Static strength of all structural softgoods shall be test verified.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The minimum design and test factors of safety for structural softgoods shall be as specified in Table 6, Minimum Design and Test Factors for Structural Softgoods.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The minimum design and test factors of safety for beryllium structures shall be as specified in Table 7, Minimum Design and Test Factors for Beryllium Structures.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: When using cross-rolled sheet, the design shall preclude out-of-plane loads and displacements during assembly, testing, or service life.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Stress analysis shall properly account for the lack of ductility of the material by rigorous treatment of applied loads, boundary conditions, assembly stresses, stress concentrations, thermal cycling, possible material anisotropy, and worst-case tolerance conditions.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: All machined and/or mechanically disturbed surfaces shall be chemically milled to ensure removal of surface damage and residual stresses.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: All parts shall undergo penetrant inspection for surface cracks and crack-like flaws per NASA-STD-6016, Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: For NASA spaceflight structures made of well-characterized materials and with sufficient load cycle data that accounts for all in-service environments, a minimum service life factor of 4.0 shall be applied to the service life for fatigue and creep life assessments.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: All structural items subjected to significant in-plane stresses (compression and/or shear) under any combination of ground loads, flight loads, or thermal loads shall be analyzed for buckling failure.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Design loads for buckling shall be ultimate loads.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: If a loading condition tends to alleviate buckling, then the unfactored load shall be used in combination with other factored loading conditions.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Buckling evaluation shall address general instability, local or panel instability, crippling, and creep.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Analyses of thin-walled shell structures subject to buckling load conditions during the service life shall account for the differences between idealized model geometry and the physical structure, including boundary conditions.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: In the event a particular factor of safety requirement of this NASA Technical Standard cannot be met for a specific spaceflight structure or hardware component, an alternative or modified approach shall be proposed to verify the strength adequacy of the design.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: A written risk assessment that justifies the use of the alternate approach shall be prepared by the organization with primary responsibility for the development of the structure or component.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: The risk assessment shall be submitted to the delegated Technical Authority for approval prior to the implementation of the alternative approach.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: If the lower factors of safety are approved by the delegated Technical Authority, a waiver shall be written that documents the rationale for this one-time exception.

rules.standard_title: Structural Design and Test Factors of Safety for Spaceflight Hardware
rules.statement: Waivers shall not be used as precedents for future mission applications.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.section_title: Load Analysis Cycles for Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: A minimum of three load analysis cycles shall be performed: Preliminary Design Load Cycle (PDLC), Final Design Load Cycle (FDLC), and Verification Load Cycle (VLC).

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Coupled loads analyses (CLA) shall be performed on spacecraft and payloads that experience the liftoff and ascent load regimes and terrestrial landing to calculate loads, accelerations, and deflections for launch through payload separation , including potential abort events.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Loads analyses shall be performed on spacecraft that experience the mission load regimes beyond payload separation to calculate loads, accelerations, and deflections.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Each load analysis cycle shall use analysis methodologies, models (payload models, LV models, spacecraft models, etc.), analysis forcing functions, and planned mission trajectory that are representative of the design at the time of the loads analysis cycle and approved by the delegated Technical Authority .

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Limit loads shall not be exceeded with 99.87/50 (99.87 percent probability at 50 percent confidence level) for nominal flight events This "3-sigma" probability of 0.9987 with 50-percent confidence is traditionally used for aerospace structures for nominal flight loads.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Interface boundary conditions shall be consistent with the coupled configuration.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Random vibration loads analysis shall only be utilized for load predictions when the dynamic model has adequate fidelity in the frequency range of the analysis.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: For random vibration loads analysis, statistically-derived peak responses shall be used for the limit loads.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.section_title: Load Regimes for Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: All critical load regimes to which the structure will be exposed shall be evaluated in the loads analyses.
rules.rationale: Spacecraft and payloads must be designed to maintain structural integrity and the required degree of functionality to ensure successful operation during all phases of the expected life cycle. Static and dynamic load environments to be encountered during assembly, testing, transportation, launch, ascent, space operations, extraterrestrial operations, descent, and landing must be considered in flight structures and systems.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: If the structure has multiple configurations during its mission, the load regimes each configuration will experience shall be identified and each configuration evaluated in the loads analysis.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Within each load regime, each source of loading shall be evaluated in the loads analysis.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.section_title: Spacecraft and Payload Preliminary Loads for Design
rules.statement: To initiate the design process, a set of preliminary design loads shall be developed and used for the sizing of primary structure, secondary structure, and components.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: For spacecraft or payloads that can experience a variety of load regimes during transport, test, and operational phases separate from the LV, the spacecraft providers shall develop load envelopes for use in preliminary design, depending on the specific mission .

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.section_title: Spacecraft and Payload Primary Structure Design
rules.statement: Design loads for preliminary sizing of primary structure shall be based on the load databases, existing load analysis results for similar payloads, or flight data.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Angular accelerations in radians per second squared (rad/sec2) about the center of mass (c.m.) shall also be included, when appropriate.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Preliminary design load factors for components shall be obtained from a mass acceleration curve (MAC) or table that specifies load factors as a function of component weight, frequency range, structural support type, or other variables.
rules.rationale: The development of a MAC should be based on previous experience with the LV and spacecraft/payload, and, if possible, incorporate results from previous transient and random vibration analyses, as well as any available flight data. The MAC is derived to represent a combined load factor for component low frequency and random vibration loads.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: The component design load factors shall include effects of quasi- steady, transient, and random loading and thus represent the total dynamic load environment for a flight event.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.section_title: Development of Spacecraft and Payload Mathematical Model for Loads
rules.statement: To support CLA, a dynamic mathematical model of the spacecraft/payload shall be developed using finite element methods.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Spacecraft dynamic mathematical models shall be developed as needed to support load predictions for spacecraft post -separation and during operational phases, depending on the specific mission.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: The load model or models shall collectively conservatively bound the response of the nonlinear hardware.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.section_title: Form of Spacecraft and Payload Model
rules.statement: The spacecraft or payload FEM shall be developed to meet all pertinent interfaces identified by the LV for loads analysis .
rules.rationale: FEMs are based on mass, stiffness, damping, and geometry. The model may be a reduced version of a FEM developed for stress analyses or may be a model developed specifically for load analysis.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Interface boundary conditions shall be consistent with the coupled configuration.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: The boundary conditions for the model shall correspond to the attachment to the supporting structure.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.section_title: Resolution and Fidelity of a Spacecraft and Payload Unreduced Model
rules.statement: The spacecraft and payload dynamic model shall have sufficient fidelity to capture the dynamic behavior of the spacecraft and payload in the defined analysis frequency range.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Subsystem resonances and overall spacecraft and payload system modes shall be modeled up to a model upper bound frequency of at least 1.5 times the cutoff frequency of the load analysis, assuming fixed boundary condition at its mounting interface.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: If dynamic reduction is used, the FEM shall be reduced to a cutoff frequency of at least 1.5 times the cutoff frequency of the load analysis (which is typically the cutoff frequency of the coupled LV/spacecraft system or LV/payload system).

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: If static reduction is used, the dynamic characteristics of the reduced model shall preserve the characteristics of the original model up to the model upper bound frequency as defined by the delegated Technical A uthority.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: To capture the static-elastic contribution of the modes above the frequency cutoff of the model either residual vectors or a mode acceleration approach shall be used in the calculation of the limit loads.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: For model output requests, the output transformation matrix (OTM) format shall be documented and provided with grid and/or element identification information with a description of the type of response (e.g., peak acceleration).

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Non-deterministic forcing functions used in spacecraft loads analysis shall be 99.87/50 (99.87 percent probability at 50 percent confidence level) levels or greater.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Forcing functions for events associated with the spacecraft shall be developed by the spacecraft provider and/or mission organization, as needed.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: For spacecraft and payload forcing functions, analytical predictions, ground test data, and flight data shall be utilized to the maximum extent possible for the forcing function definition.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: For spacecraft and payload forcing functions, the effects of variations and tolerances of parameters that govern the forcing functions shall be considered in the analysis methodology.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: For the Monte Carlo approach, an appropriate distribution shall be defined for each parameter so a value can be randomly selected.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Damping used for DLA shall be based on test measurements of the actual structure, at amplitude levels that are representative of actual flight environments, or on experience with similar types of structures whenever possible.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: In the absence of measured damping data, damping of one percent (1 percent) of critical shall be assumed for each mode for CLA.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: For random vibration load analysis, damping shall be specified per the delegated Technical Authority, in the absence of measured damping data.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.section_title: Spacecraft and Payload and Spacecraft Uncertainty Factors
rules.statement: To reduce design impacts associated with load changes, uncertainty factors shall be approved for each loads analysis cycle by the delegated Technical Authority and applied to DLA results.
rules.rationale: Coupled loads analysis has uncertainty associated with both the LV and spacecraft inputs to the CLA, which are typically accounted for by using uncertainty factors. From the LV perspective, the uncertainty is due primarily to using approximate forcing functions and unverified models in the CLA, especially for vehicles with no flight history.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: In subsequent load cycles, pending approval from the delegated Technical Authority, the uncertainty factor shall be gradually reduced as the structural design and load analysis prediction mature.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: If the model verification and validation is determined to be inadequate by the delegated Technical Authority, an uncertainty factor higher than 1.1 shall be retained in the verification load analysis cycle.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: In cases where loads produced by different environments can occur simultaneously, these loads shall be combined statistically to define the limit load for each time/event period in the load regime of interest.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Combinations of these loads occur at different times in flight and shall be examined for each flight event.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Loads from each load regime contributor shall be combined in a statistically appropriate manner.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: The statistical distributions of each load regime contributor shall describe the peaks of the loads.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: The quasi-static, low frequency transient loads, and random vibration/acoustic limit loads shall be combined as approved by the delegated Technical Authority to determine the total load environment for liftoff.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: When an RSS approach to defining component loads is utilized, the maximum low frequency load factor and maximum random vibration load factor shall be root-sum- squared (RSS'd).

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: When a time-consistent/load regime-consistent approach to defining component loads is utilized, the low frequency transient load factor and random vibration load factor shall be combined in a time-consistent/load regime-consistent manner.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Inertial forces in all three axes, including rotations if appropriate, shall be applied simultaneously, including sign combinations.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Prior to testing, an accurate mass representation of the test article shall be demonstrated with orthogonality checks using the reduced mass matrix [MA] and the analytical mode shapes.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: The off-diagonal terms of the self-orthogonality matrix shall be less than 0.1 for all target/significant modes with the on-diagonal terms equal to 1.0.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Validation of the spacecraft and payload dynamic models by modal survey testing shall be performed to ensure the model is sufficiently accurate for load and deflection predictions.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: The modal survey test shall measure, with sufficient accuracy, all target/significant modes below the model upper bound frequency and provide test data supporting the model correlation requirements of this section.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: If only a subset of modes can be measured, then the target/significant modes shall include at least the first two primary bending modes, first axial, first torsion, and all modes that introduce significant strain energy into primary interfaces or components.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: The modal survey test shall be performed as a fixed-base test, free-free test with interfaces appropriately inertially loaded, or use an appropriate fixed-base correction method that incorporates excitation at the interfaces.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: For any modal survey test, propellant tanks shall be appropriately mass loaded.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Sufficient instrumentation shall be included across the boundary interface(s) (on both the test article and the supporting structure) to characterize and verify linear boundary behavior in all six DOFs throughout the frequency range of interest.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: The modal survey test shall include techniques approved by the delegated Technical Authority to identify nonlinearities.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Agreement between test and analysis natural frequencies (after model correlation) shall be within 5 percent for the target/significant modes.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Accurate mass representation of the test article shall be demonstrated with orthogonality checks using the analytical mass matrix [MA] and the test mode shapes [øT] for the target/significant modes.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: The off-diagonal terms of the orthogonality matrix shall be less than 0.1 for all target/significant modes based on the diagonal terms normalized to 1.0.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Mode shape comparisons shall be made via cross-orthogonality checks using the test modes [øT], the analytical modes [øA], and the analytical mass matrix [MA] for target/significant modes.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: The absolute value of the cross-orthogonality between corresponding test and analytical mode shapes shall be a minimum of 0.9 and all other terms of the matrix less than 0.1 for all significant modes.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: All modeling adjustments or changes made to achieve the above-stated criteria shall be consistent with the as-built hardware and its drawings.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Qualitative comparisons between test modes and analytical modes using mode shape animation and/or deflection plots shall be performed.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: Modal damping shall be obtained for each mode measured.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: A measurement of the test configuration mass and CG along with exact configuration notes shall be included as part of the modal test report.

rules.standard_title: Load Analyses of Spacecraft and Payloads
rules.statement: For FEMs, the model quality checks listed below shall be performed:

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Tailoring of the requirements in this NASA Technical Standard for application to a specific program or project shall be formally documented as part of program or project requirements and approved by the delegated Technical Authority in accordance with NPR 7120.5, NASA Space Flight Program and Project Management Requirements.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The latest issuances of cited documents shall apply unless specific versions are designated.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Non-use of specifically designated versions shall be approved by the delegated Technical Authority.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Conflicts between this NASA Technical Standard and other requirements documents shall be resolved by the delegated Technical Authority.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.section_title: Specification of this NASA Technical Standard on Contracts
rules.statement: When this NASA Technical Standard is specified on contract documents, a detailed weld process specification, as defined in NPR 7120.10, which meets the intent of this NASA Technical Standard shall be submitted.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Welding performed using this NASA Technical Standard shall be classified in accordance with the service of the joints as follows in the next sections.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Class A welds shall require visual, dimensional, surface, and volumetric inspections, and additional inspection when required by engineering drawing.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Class B welds shall require visual, dimensional, and surface inspections, and additional inspection when required by engineering drawing.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Class B welds shall be subjected to volumetric inspection if required by engineering design and specified by drawing or special instruction.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Weld requiring fail-safe capability shall be classified as a Class B joint.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Class C welds shall require visual and dimensional inspections, and additional inspection when required by engineering drawing.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Class C joints shall require weld integrity verification based on function of the joint (e.g., seal welds require leak testing commensurate with the leak rate requirement).

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Automatic, semiautomatic, manual, and machine welding shall be accomplished using equipment containing calibrated dials, meters, or recorders that quantitatively indicate process parameters.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: All joining equipment (including manual) shall be capable of producing joints that meet the requirements specified herein.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: New, repaired, relocated, or modified welding machines and equipment for automatic and machine welding shall be acceptance-tested prior to processing of flight hardware.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Machines and equipment shall meet the requirements of the applicable purchase specification, design specification, or modification order.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Power supplies and supporting components (electrical or mechanical or both) shall be capable of operating reliably within the range of parameters and duty cycle to be used for joining production parts.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Measuring instruments, meters, gages, or direct reading electrical control circuits to be used for automatic, semiautomatic, and machine joining operations shall be initially calibrated and periodically recalibrated to maintain adequate performance.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Maintenance performed on measuring instruments, meters, gages, or direct reading electrical control circuits to be used for automatic, semiautomatic, and machine performance joining operations shall require recalibration to maintain adequate performance.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Measuring instruments, meters, gages, or direct reading electrical control circuits to be used for automatic, semiautomatic, and machine joining operations shall be initially calibrated and periodically recalibrated to maintain adequate performance or when any maintenance is performed that may have changed calibration.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Welding machines shall be provided with adequate periodic maintenance service so that acceptable welds can be produced using qualified welding procedure specifications.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: A current record of each maintenance repair or functional check shall be maintained for each welding machine.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Tooling and fixtures used in the joining operation shall be constructed of nonmagnetic materials that do not affect the welding arc or beam, or that are not detrimental to the weld quality.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Tooling and fixtures shall not be a source of contamination of the joint.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Magnetic materials, when used, shall be degaussed prior to welding.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Degaussing, when necessary, shall be controlled by the WPS for the successful completion of the weld.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Tooling and fixtures required to ensure compliance with dimensional requirements of section 4.8.3 shall be identified on the WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Unless otherwise specified or approved by the procuring agency, base metal alloy shall conform to applicable government and/or industry specifications for each alloy group.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The base metal, material condition, and appropriate specification shall be recorded in the WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Weld start and runoff tabs, when used, shall be of the same alloy as the material being joined and be welded with the same filler metal specified on the drawing or WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Unless otherwise specified or approved by the procuring agency, filler metal alloy shall conform to applicable government and/or industry specifications for each alloy group.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Specifications used to procure filler metals shall include provisions to mitigate the possibility of two different filler wires being errantly mixed together on a single spool or in a filler rod container.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Weld filler metals and the appropriate specifications shall be recorded in the WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Welding-grade gases conforming to the applicable industry or military specifications shall be used for gas shielding.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The shield gas type, specification, and flow rate shall be recorded in the WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Tungsten electrodes shall conform to the applicable industry or military specifications.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The electrode diameter, electrode tip shape, alloy composition, and specification shall be recorded as a part of the WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Pin and shoulder service life shall be demonstrated to meet the intended use.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Pins and shoulders shall be limited to the demonstrated life.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Pin tool design, materials, and service life shall be recorded in the WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Pins and shoulders that have reached the specified service life shall be marked and removed from service to preclude an accidental future use in the FSW production process.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: If used for more than one weld joint, pins and shoulders shall be cleaned and inspected before reuse on production hardware.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Operators of automatic, semiautomatic, machine, or manual welding equipment shall be certified for the applicable process.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Suppliers shall define the weld certification process in their detailed weld process specification.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Prior to first production of parts, or when changes are made to essential variables of the WPS, qualification joints shall be made to establish a satisfactory WPS for each different configuration of A, B, and C classes of welds.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Variables considered essential shall be so identified in the WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Classes A and B joints shall be qualified with joints that simulate the production part with respect to section thickness, alloy, heat-treat condition, joint preparation, pre-weld cleaning, fit-up, position, and post-weld operations.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The joints shall be processed in either the actual production fixture or in a test fixture simulating the production fixture using the production welding equipment.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Base metal for qualification joining tests shall be identified by lot or heat number, type, and condition and maintain identification through all evaluation processes.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The qualification weld shall be subjected to metallurgical evaluation and the same post-weld inspections and processes as the production parts, including reinforcement removal, mechanical deformation, stress relief, and thermal treatments associated with artificial aging or any operation affecting mechanical properties.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: As a minimum, all essential joining variables (such as voltage, current, rate of travel, position, and filler-wire feed rate) shall be recorded during qualification welding.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Manual weld parameters and operating parameter ranges shall be established during the WPS qualification.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The WPS shall document all pre-welding operations, setup conditions, welding equipment, and any pertinent information about the welding system used which affects the joining operation.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: For automatic, semiautomatic, and machine joining, parameter tolerances may be used and shall be listed in the qualified WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Test samples representing the minimum and maximum heat input shall be processed to verify acceptable welds and the results documented in the PQR.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: All test and inspection results used to verify the weld integrity shall be recorded on the PQR.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Records of test specimens that meet the acceptance requirements of this process specification shall be signed and dated by a Quality Assurance (QA) representative as an accurate record of the welding and testing of the procedure test weldment.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The WPS and PQR shall be prepared and retained as long-term temporary records in accordance with NPR 1441.1, NASA Records Retention Schedules, with the current WPS being maintained at the welding station.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: All WPSs and PQRs shall be maintained and made available for review by the responsible NASA Engineering Authority before production of hardware covered under this NASA Technical Standard.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: All joints shall be documented on a WPS, design drawing, or other suitable document.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Pre-weld cleaning of filler materials and surfaces to be welded to remove contaminants that are detrimental to weld quality shall be accomplished in an environment that will not degrade the quality of the weld.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Cleanliness shall be maintained during welding.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Pre-weld and interpass cleaning requirements shall be included in the WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: A welding equipment operational readiness check shall be made immediately prior to a production weld to verify the equipment is operating properly.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The equipment operational readiness check criteria shall be provided to the procuring agency.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Pre-heat, interpass, and post-heat temperatures shall be controlled so as not to degrade the properties of the material being welded.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The parameters of pre-heat, interpass, and post-heat temperatures shall be recorded in an applicable WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Tack welding is allowable and shall either be removed or become a part of the finished weld (i.e., tack welds are to be completely consumed by the final weldment).

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Tack welds that become part of the finished weld shall be performed by certified welders in accordance with certified procedures meeting the requirements of this NASA Technical Standard.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: After the weldment is completed, the tack areas shall be evaluated to the requirements of the finished weld.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Tack welding requirements shall be included in the WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The technique of welding the initial passes from both sides where the weld roots overlap beneath the exposed surfaces (reference Figure 2 (A), Welding Techniques) shall be permitted only if the root of the first pass is removed to sound metal prior to placement of the first weld pass from the second side.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Joints which have prepared grooves from one or both sides (reference Figure 2 (B) and (C)) and/or multi-pass welds shall have a weld land that is completely penetrated on the initial pass.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Adequate nondestructive evaluation (NDE) procedures shall be employed to ensure that the weld root has been exposed by machining.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: All penetration weld passes shall have no visual evidence of improper fusion or presence of dross.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Square groove welds shall be completely penetrated from one side (reference Figure 2 (D)).

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The technique of welding and joint geometry shall be as stated on the engineering drawing and the WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Any deviation regarding weld technique and joint geometry shall be approved by the procuring agent prior to use.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Partial penetration groove welds shall be used only for Class C joints.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Production welding shall be accomplished according to a qualified WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Departure from the qualified WPS during production welding shall require withholding the part for MRB disposition.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The cause for departure shall be determined.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Corrective action shall be taken prior to further production welding.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Each completed weldment, including the base metal, shall be inspected to ensure compliance with the requirements of sections 4.8.2, 4.8.3, and 4.10, and as dictated by the class of weld for a minimum of 12.5 mm (0.5 in) on either side of the weld interface.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Weld deposits, buildup, and root reinforcement shall comply with the criteria outlined in the accompanying detailed weld process specification that is submitted in support of this NASA Technical Standard.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The edge of the weld deposit shall blend into the base metal without unfused overlaps or undercut.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Weld face and root sides shall be free of surface cracks, crater cracks, and other defects open to the surface.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Weld deposits shall be free of open voids or unfused overlapping folds or other lack of fusion.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Undercutting, concavity, lack of fill, or root concavity shall be unacceptable in any weld where it occurs as a sharp notch or where the depth reduces the material thickness below the minimum thickness specified on the applicable drawing.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: If not specifically addressed in drawing tolerances or by specified welding standards, allowable post-weld mismatch shall be governed by overall drawing tolerances.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: If not specifically addressed in drawing tolerances or by specified welding standards, allowable post-weld peaking of the welded joint and adjacent base metal shall be governed by overall drawing tolerances.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: A standard template or other device having specified reference points shall be used for determination of peaking.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Weld bead reinforcement removal shall not thin the weld or parent metal below drawing dimensional requirements.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: When flush contour is required by the welding symbol, weld reinforcement shall not exceed 0.4 mm (0.015 in).

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Metal removal shall be such that the reworked area blends smoothly (e.g., 3.175 mm (0.125 in) radius) with adjacent material without abrupt sectional changes.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Weldments that are machined ground or otherwise mechanically worked causing disruption or smearing of the material surface shall be etched to remove the masking material before penetrant application.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Fillet weld fusion of the root shall have a minimum of 10 percent penetration of base metal thickness of the thinnest member of the root of the joint as determined by evaluation of transverse sections taken from the qualification welds.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The minimum penetration shall be verified by destructive test/metallography.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Weld parameters used to successfully and repeatedly complete the fillet welds shall be entered into the WPS and used for actual welding.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Fillet weld fusion of the root (reference Figure 4, Fillet Welds) shall be determined by evaluation of transverse sections taken from the qualification welds.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Intermittent fillet welds shall have fusion of the root throughout the specified length.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The minimum acceptable fillet size shall be that specified by the engineering drawing.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Unless otherwise specified on the engineering drawing, the maximum acceptable fillet size shall be the size specified plus 50 percent or 4.8 mm (0.188 in), whichever is less, as permitted in section 4.11.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The minimum acceptable actual throat shall equal or exceed the theoretical throat (reference Figure 4).

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Prior to implementation, however, verification by NDE, destructive testing, and metallurgical evaluation that the process used for straightening does not degrade the weld or surrounding material below the specified design requirements shall be performed.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Following weldment straightening, the weld and adjacent base metal shall be inspected in accordance with section 4.8.1.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Weldments in which defects caused by weldment straightening are revealed shall not be acceptable.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Weldments that are subject to heat treatment operations shall be subsequently inspected to the surface quality requirements of the engineering drawing.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Any required post-weld heat treatment processing shall be specified in the WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: If not otherwise specified in the design requirements, weld strength shall meet or exceed that of the parent material.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Qualified WPSs shall be established to demonstrate the weld meets the strength required by design.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Unless otherwise directed by the procuring agency, fillet weld shear strength shall meet or exceed 60 percent of the minimum ultimate tensile requirements of the weld.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: For fillet weld joints involving materials of different thicknesses having different ultimate tensile strength values, the minimum requirement for the shear joint shall be 60 percent of the lower of the minimum ultimate tensile requirements.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Weldment quality requirements shall be established to ensure the weld meets design requirements for strength and integrity.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The compliance of the weld with quality requirements shall be verified by mechanical testing.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Additional welding operations shall be permitted to correct any unacceptable condition established per section 4.10, provided the repair welding parameters and procedures are specified in a qualified repair WPS, and the repair is contained within the original weld zone.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Complete records of the repair welding operation, including identification of the repaired weldment, type of defect, and location of the repair weld, shall be retained in permanent records.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: No more than two weld repair attempts shall be made without approval of the MRB.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Reinspection of all repair weld areas shall be performed using the same methods/requirements as the original weld.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Except as otherwise specified, the supplier shall use inspection facilities.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Except as otherwise specified, services approved by the procuring agency shall be used by the supplier.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Inspection and test records shall be kept complete and, upon request, made available to the procuring agency or its designated representative.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: NDE procedures to be employed in inspection for weldment internal and surface quality requirements shall be qualified/validated as being capable of detecting the weldment quality criteria prescribed prior to inspection of the first production weld.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The documented proof of capability shall be retained as a permanent record.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Personnel performing visual weld inspections shall be certified in accordance with AWS QC1, Standard for AWS Certification of Welding Inspectors, or an equivalent standard as determined by the Quality Authority.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Personnel performing NDE weld inspections shall be certified in accordance with NAS 410, NASA Certification and Qualification of Nondestructive Test Personnel, or an equivalent standard as determined by the Quality Authority.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Documentation relative to the production weld shall be reviewed for conformance to section 4.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Each production weld shall be certified that it was made within the range of operating parameters established in the WPS.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Any deviations from operating parameters established in WPS shall be noted and referred to the procuring agency for disposition.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Each completed weldment, including the base metal, shall be inspected to ensure compliance with the requirements of sections 4.8.2, 4.8.3, 4.10, and as dictated by the class of weld for a minimum of 12.5 mm (0.5 in) on either side of the weld interface.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: The weld shall be in the as-welded condition for the initial inspection, with surface smut and loose oxide removed using a technique that does not smear metal or change the quality of the weld.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Titanium weld deposit and heat-affected zone shall adhere to the color requirements of the accompanying detailed weld process specification identified in section 1.2.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Dimensional inspection shall be performed on weldments to ensure compliance with the requirements of the design drawing for all weld classes.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: NDE shall be performed to ensure compliance with the internal quality requirements of the design drawing established per section 4.10 for Class A, B, and C welds as noted in Figure 5, Minimum Inspection Requirements.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: NDE procedures and techniques shall be qualified.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: NDE shall be performed to ensure compliance with the surface quality requirements of the design drawing for Class A and Class B welds.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: NDE procedures shall be qualified.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: When reliability of inspection and critical flaw detection so dictate, redundant and/or complementing inspection techniques and procedures shall be employed.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Machined, ground, or otherwise mechanically worked weldments that have been subject to smearing of the weld material surface shall be etched to remove the masking material prior to penetrant application.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: A continuous audit of weldment production quality shall be maintained.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Weldment production quality audit records shall include, but not be limited to, the location of repairs, type of defects repaired, procedures used, and inches of repair per total inches of weld.

rules.standard_title: General Welding Requirements for Aerospace Materials
rules.statement: Audit records of weldment production quality shall be made available to the procuring agency upon request.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Standard NDE shall consist of nondestructive inspections of traditional (non-AM) metal components (e.g., aluminum, steel, titanium, and nickel alloys) using the NDE methods cited in Table 1, Reliably Detectable Flaw Sizes for Fracture Analysis Based on Standard NDE Methods (Millimeters), or Table 2, Reliably Detectable Flaw Sizes for Fracture Analysis Based on Standard NDE Methods (Inches), or additional methods that have been demonstrated by section 4.1.5 in this Standard and approved by the RFCB or delegated NASA Technical Authority.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Nondestructive inspections of fracture-critical hardware shall detect the initial crack sizes used in the damage tolerance fracture analyses with a capability of minimum 90% probability of detection at a 95% confidence level, known as 90/95.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: The justification to classify an NDE procedure as a Standard NDE procedure shall be documented and approved by NDE engineering and the delegated NASA Technical Authority.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Instrument standardization shall be performed.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Eddy-current inspections shall be in accordance with SAE ARP4402, Eddy Current Inspection of Open Fastener Holes in Aluminum Aircraft Structure; SAE AS4787, Eddy Current Inspection of Circular Holes in Nonferrous Metallic Aircraft Engine Hardware; or NASA fracture control and NASA NDE Engineering-approved contractor internal specifications with the following additional requirements:

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: For part conductivities less than 10% International Annealed Copper Standard (IACS), the reference standard shall have a conductivity of ±2% IACS with a minimum reference standard conductivity of 0.8% IACS.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Reference standard notches used for standardization shall be no larger than ½ the length and ½ the depth of the target crack size with a maximum width of 0.127 mm (0.005 in): The shape of surface and through artificial notches can be anywhere between semi-circular "thumb nail" to rectangular.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Noise levels on the component shall be less than 25% of the reference notch response.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Any indication greater than 50% of the reference notch response shall be reported to, and receive disposition by, the proper engineering authority.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: The influence of coatings and lift-off variations on the reliability of an eddy current Standard NDE inspection process shall be evaluated for application-specific suitability and documented in the NDE Summary Report.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Fluorescent Penetrant inspection shall be in accordance with ASTM E1417/E1417M, Standard Practice for Liquid Penetrant Testing; SAE AMS2647, Fluorescent Penetrant Inspection Aircraft Structures and Engine Component Maintenance; or NASA fracture control and NASA NDE Engineering-approved contractor internal specifications.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: The penetrant system used shall be a fluorescent penetrant of sensitivity level 3 or 4. (See NASA/TM-2011-215869, A Comparison of the Capability of Sensitivity Level 3 and Sensitivity Level 4 Fluorescent Penetrants to Detect Fatigue Cracks in Various Metals.) 4.1.4.2.2 Mechanically Disturbed Surfaces

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Mechanically disturbed or wire EDM surfaces shall be etched or electropolished prior to the penetrant inspection and at an appropriate time in the manufacturing flow.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: An etching/electropolishing procedure shall be developed, approved, and controlled to prevent part damage.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: The etching/electropolishing procedure shall specify the minimum amount of material to be removed to ensure that smeared metal does not mask cracks.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Non-ferrous materials such as aluminum and titanium alloys shall be etched/electropolished to remove a minimum of 0.01 mm (0.0004 in) of material.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Corrosion resistant steel and nickel-based alloys shall be etched/electropolished to remove a minimum of 0.005 mm (0.0002 in) of material.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: If etching/electropolishing is not feasible or the minimum depths are not attainable, it shall be demonstrated and documented that the required flaw size can be reliably detected following current machining processes.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Interpretation of reliably detectable flaw sizes for thin parts with thickness less than 0.64 mm (0.025 in) or edges and holes with a fillet radius shall require approval by the RFCB.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Magnetic particle inspections shall be in accordance with ASTM E1444/E1444M, Standard Practice for Magnetic Particle Testing for Aerospace, or NASA fracture control and NASA NDE Engineering-approved contractor internal specifications.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: The magnetic particle inspection shall be the wet, fluorescent, continuous, or multi-mag method.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: A Quantitative Quality Indicator (QQI) shall be used to validate the local field intensities.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Type CX-230 or CX4-230 QQIs shall be used on curved or complex surfaces where the Type CX-430 or CX4-430 QQIs cannot conform to the part in the area of interest.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: A POD study shall be used to establish the capability of digital radiographic techniques, including computed radiography, digital radiography, and computed tomography which at this time are considered to be Special NDE.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: A POD study shall be used to establish the capability of Gamma radiation source inspections which are considered special NDE.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Film radiographic inspections, with capabilities shown in Table 1 or Table 2, shall be in accordance with ASTM E1742/E1742M, Standard Practice for Radiographic Examination, or NASA fracture control and NASA NDE Engineering-approved contractor internal specifications with the following additional requirements:

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: The minimum radiographic inspection sensitivity level shall be 2-1T.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Film density shall be 2.5 to 4.0.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: The local x-ray axis of the radiation beam shall be within ±5 degrees of the assumed crack plane orientation.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Utrasonic inspections for wrought products shall be in accordance with ASTM E2375, Standard Practice for Ultrasonic Testing of Wrought Products; ASTM E164, Standard Practice for Contact Ultrasonic Testing of Weldments; or NASA fracture control and NASA NDE Engineering-approved contractor internal specifications with the following additional requirements:

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
rules.statement: Any single discontinuity with a response greater than 50% of the response from a 1.98-mm (0.078-in) diameter flat bottom hole (or equivalent surface notch or side drilled hole) at the estimated discontinuity depth (or sound path) shall be reported.

rules.standard_title: Nondestructive Evaluation Requirements for Fracture-Critical Metallic Components
