rules.statement: Gold-aluminum bonding processes shall be controlled to prevent the formation of the AuAl2 intermetallic known as "Purple Plague" and the Au5Al2 intermetallic known as "White Plague." 4.2.6.6 Fastener Installation 4.2.6.6.1 Liquid Locking Compounds

rules.standard_title: Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft
rules.statement: If a liquid-locking compound is used as a locking feature where rotational loosening or disengagement would result in a critical or catastrophic hazard, its use shall comply with the design and quality requirements and best practices in NASA-STD-5020, Requirements for Threaded Fastening Systems in Spaceflight Hardware, sections 5.5 and 7.6 and Appendix C.

rules.standard_title: Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft
rules.statement: Silver-plated fasteners shall not be used in external applications where the silver plating is directly exposed to atomic oxygen for a period longer than 2 weeks.

rules.standard_title: Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft
rules.statement: A Contamination Control Plan shall be generated in accordance with the guidelines of ASTM E1548 (2009), Standard Practice for Preparation of Aerospace Contamination Control Plans.

rules.standard_title: Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft
rules.statement: The Contamination Control Plan shall include controls on contamination- sensitive manufacturing processes, such as adhesive bonding, controls on packaging for shipment and storage, and a foreign object damage/debris (FOD) prevention program.

rules.standard_title: Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft
rules.statement: The FOD prevention program shall be established for all ground operations of mechanical and electrical systems of spaceflight hardware, including the design, development, manufacturing, assembly, repair, processing, testing, maintenance, operation, and checkout of the equipment to ensure the highest practical level of cleanliness.

rules.standard_title: Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft
rules.statement: The FOD prevention program shall conform to NAS 412 (Revision 1), Foreign Object Damage/Foreign Object Debris (FOD) Prevention.

rules.standard_title: Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft
rules.statement: Cleanliness levels for assembly- and subassembly-level hardware shall be identified on the engineering drawings.

rules.standard_title: Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft
rules.statement: Packaging shall protect spaceflight hardware from corrosion and contamination during shipping and storage.

rules.standard_title: Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft
rules.statement: Verification of compliance with the requirements of this NASA Technical Standard shall consist of the following steps, as a minimum: NOTE: Per section 4.1.6: The use of M&P that do not comply with the requirements of this NASA Technical Standard may be acceptable in the actual hardware applications.

rules.standard_title: Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft
rules.statement: Materials used in nitrogen tetroxide systems shall be evaluated for flammability in nitrogen tetroxide using promoted combustion tests similar to NASA-STD- 6001B, Test 17, for metallic materials used in oxygen systems and Test 1 for polymeric materials.

rules.standard_title: Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft
rules.statement: When materials are determined to be flammable, a nitrogen tetroxide compatibility assessment shall be conducted using the methodology described for oxygen systems in NASA-STD-6001B and the system safety rationale of this assessment documented in an MUA.

rules.standard_title: Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft
rules.statement: Materials used in other oxidizer systems shall be evaluated for potential ignition.

rules.standard_title: Standard Materials and Processes Requirements for Spacecraft
rules.statement: NDE acceptance criteria and requirements for qualification of NDE procedures used on all (metallic, non-metallic and composite) hardware shall be addressed in the NDE Plan.
